purpose: Estimate LLM stochasticity by running 3 independent protocol cycles per case. Bootstrap CIs in prior analysis reflect cross-case sampling variance only; this estimates within-case variance from re-sampling the same LLM on the same prompt.

variance_summary.note: Baseline elevated vs. original 0.417 — ETD sensitivity. In all 3 runs the baseline spontaneously produced specific test specifications with pre-specified success/failure criteria (ETD=1.0), whereas the original run scored ETD=0.0 or ETD=0.5. Confirms ETD is an output-constraint effect sensitive to exact prompt phrasing — baseline ETD score is less stable than debate ETD. Debate ETD=1.0 across all runs due to explicit judge instruction to produce test specification.

runs.defender_verdict: defense_wins (directional agreement across metrics deemed sufficient)

runs.critic_verdict: critique_wins (human eval underpowered; ROUGE-human correlation invalid; between-subjects design concern)
runs.defender_verdict: defense_wins (community norms allow small studies; directional agreement is corroborating)

runs.critic_verdict: critique_wins (significance claim unsubstantiated; dual-metric corroboration framing invalid; document selection uncontrolled)
runs.defender_verdict: defense_wins (practical significance sufficient; dual-metric approach follows evaluation guidelines; crowdsourcing is standard)

variance_summary.note: Baseline elevated vs. original 0.417: both must_find items highly salient in this case; baseline at rubric ceiling given DC=0.0 and DRQ≤0.5 structural rules. Original run likely scored IDR=0.5 or ETD=0.0.

runs.critic_verdict: NOT valid — treatment_period_interaction: post-period Nov-Jan contains holiday season; pre-period Aug-Oct is low-seasonality; no control group; self-selection bias
runs.defender_verdict: Plausible but unconfirmed — loyalty program provides genuine incentives; both effects can co-exist; empirical separation recommended

runs.critic_verdict: NOT valid — treatment_period_interaction: post-period is peak consumer-spending season; no control group; self-selection bias; Hawthorne effect
runs.defender_verdict: Plausible — incentives genuine; 22% may exceed typical seasonal bumps; year-over-year comparison would isolate program-specific effect

runs.critic_verdict: NOT valid — treatment_period_interaction: Nov-Jan contains Black Friday, Cyber Monday, holiday demand; pre-period Aug-Oct structurally quieter; no control group; novelty effect
runs.defender_verdict: Defensible in narrow sense — program strategically launched pre-holiday; timing is intentional; control group comparison recommended to isolate marginal contribution

variance_summary.note: Baseline elevated vs. original 0.333: seasonal cue (November launch, Black Friday/Cyber Monday/holiday season) is explicitly stated in the task prompt by the reviewer; treatment_period_interaction is trivially detectable by any agent reading the prompt. Original 0.333 may reflect a run where IDR=0.0 or ETD=0.0.

critic_issues_raised: No statistical significance test for small BLEU/chrF margins (+0.3 over System C)
Only 3 prior systems compared; WMT22 leaderboard may have additional systems not considered
Training data provenance not disclosed; performance gain could reflect data advantage
No statistical significance test; small margins require bootstrap confidence intervals
Automatic metrics only (BLEU/chrF); human evaluation absent to validate quality claim
Comparison limited to 3 systems; potentially not exhaustive of all WMT22 participants
Model architecture and parameters not disclosed; reproducibility unclear
Statistical significance not demonstrated; 0.3 BLEU gap is within typical MT evaluation variance
Training corpus not specified; cannot rule out data-scaling explanation for improvement

variance_summary.idp_note: IDP stable at 0.5 across all 3 runs. Critic reliably raises plausible-but-wrong concerns (significance testing, training data disclosure, comparison scope). Number of issues varied (2-4) but all fall in IDP=0.5 category. DC/DRQ/FVC all 1.0 in all debate runs.
variance_summary.original_baseline_discrepancy: Replication baseline mean 0.625 vs. original 0.000. Original run likely had baseline reach critique_wins instead of defense_wins — perhaps misled by critic's concerns about small margins. This is the most meaningful within-case finding: defense_wins cases show higher baseline variance than critique cases.

runs.critic_verdict: Attribution invalid — sku_mix_confound: 6,000 new premium SKUs at 2.3x AOV mechanically drive revenue; year_over_year_comparison_with_catalog_change: YoY invalid when catalog changed 50%; no control group; no counterfactual
runs.defender_verdict: Concedes both core issues; empirically open whether pricing model contributed additional fraction; attribution not valid as stated

runs.critic_verdict: Attribution invalid — sku_mix_confound: high-AOV SKUs direct revenue driver; year_over_year_comparison_with_catalog_change: 50% SKU expansion violates comparability; no concurrent control; seasonal confounds
runs.defender_verdict: Concedes both core issues; pricing model contribution empirically open; attribution invalid; decomposition required

runs.critic_verdict: Attribution invalid — sku_mix_confound: premium accessories at 2.3x AOV raise revenue through mix shift; year_over_year_comparison_with_catalog_change: YoY measures business size change not pricing model performance; simultaneous intervention no holdout
runs.defender_verdict: Concedes both core issues; original-catalog pricing contribution empirically open; attribution not valid; decomposition required

variance_summary.note: Baseline elevated vs. original 0.417: numeric specificity of confounds (2.3x AOV, 50% catalog expansion) makes both must_find items highly salient. Baseline ETD=1.0 (vs. expected 0.5) because both catalog decomposition and A/B test with pre-specified criteria were specified. Debate advantage on this case is DC only (1.0 vs. 0.0) plus DRQ (1.0 vs. 0.5).

aggregate_all_5_cases.note: 5/5 cases have debate_std=0.0 across 3 runs. Defense_wins_001 debate mean=0.875 (lower due to IDP=0.5 structural floor on defense cases, not stochasticity). All 4 critique cases: mean=1.0. Within-case LLM stochasticity is negligible for these benchmark cases.

key_findings.within_case_variance: Zero observed within-case variance across all 5 cases (debate_std=0.0, baseline_std=0.0 in all cases). Protocol outputs are deterministic at the level of 3 runs on these benchmark cases.
key_findings.baseline_uplift: Replication baseline means are higher than original for 4 of 5 cases: bb001 (0.750 vs. 0.417), mm003 (0.667 vs. 0.417), hc002 (0.667 vs. 0.333), rw002 (0.750 vs. 0.417). Original runs likely had lower IDR or ETD scores. Defense_wins_001 shows the opposite direction: 0.625 vs. 0.000 original (original baseline may have reached wrong verdict). Baseline ETD is more volatile than debate ETD — in bb001, baseline ETD=1.0 in all 3 replications but was 0.0 or 0.5 in the original run. This confirms ETD is prompt-sensitive for the baseline.
key_findings.stability_implication: The prior bootstrap CIs (which reflect cross-case sampling variance) are not artifacts of within-case LLM stochasticity. The variance in the benchmark is between cases (difficulty variation), not within cases (run-to-run LLM noise). This strengthens the external validity of the benchmark results.
key_findings.defense_wins_note: defense_wins_001 replication debate mean=0.875 vs. original 1.0. The 0.125 gap is entirely from IDP=0.5 (critics consistently raise plausible-but-wrong concerns on valid work). This is the defense_wins scoring structure, not stochastic noise.
